value_picks.name: Cognizant Technology Solutions

fcf_yield.detail: FCF $1.909B / mcap $24.8B = 7.70% — clears the 7% pass threshold.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 8.52 vs implied PE 12.19 (target_median 75 / fwd_eps 6.1539) = 30.1% discount, just at the pass line.

drawdown.detail: Down 39.76% from 52w high of $87.03 — clears 35% pass threshold.

dividend.detail: Dividend yield 2.52% with payout ratio 27.3% — comfortably sustainable.

institutional.detail: Institutional ownership 105.8% (yfinance quirk, effectively >75%), rec_mean 2.17, 13/29 buy or strong_buy.

ai_exposure.detail: Dual-edged: AI/automation could compress traditional staff-augmentation services revenue, but Cognizant explicitly markets AI consulting and platform integration. PEG 0.87 and a low forward multiple suggest the market is already pricing in AI disruption risk.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest 7.55% with 14.6% MoM increase signals bearish positioning, but a 6.56-day cover ratio creates squeeze potential on any positive catalyst given the deep value setup.

catalysts_general: Trading within $0.15 of 52w low ($52.28) — extreme technical oversold
Forward PE 8.52 vs analyst implied PE ~12.2 = ~43% upside to median target $75
FCF yield 7.7% with 27% payout ratio leaves ample room for buybacks/dividend hikes
Net cash position despite 39.8% drawdown — balance sheet not the issue

downside_scenarios.scenario: Mild bear: continued IT services budget freeze in BFSI/Health Sciences segments
AI-driven offshore staff augmentation pricing pressure materializes earlier than expected
Recessionary US/Europe enterprise IT spend cuts of 8-10% combined with FX headwinds

ideal_entry.reasoning: Current price already near 52w low; entry at $51 buys ~3% below current with FCF yield ~7.9% and PE under 8.3x. Adding scale on any retest of $50 level given net cash and 2.5% dividend support.

comparables.note: Trades at large premium to CTSH despite similar growth — suggests CTSH multiple compression has overshot.
Wipro carries a meaningful premium; CTSH discount appears excessive.
ACN is the closer-quality peer in the input, also drawn down 45%; CTSH trades at a 30% PE discount to ACN despite similar margin profile.

value_picks.analyst_take: All six framework criteria pass — a rare clean sweep. Trading at the absolute 52w low with 7.7% FCF yield, net cash, sustainable 2.5% dividend, and 26 analysts averaging 2.17 conviction with $75 median target = ~43% upside. The setup pays you to wait while you assess whether AI is genuinely disrupting the IT services model or just compressing multiples temporarily.

value_picks.name: EPAM Systems, Inc.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 8.07 vs implied PE 13.87 (target_median 193 / fwd_eps 13.92) = 41.8% discount.

drawdown.detail: Down 49.52% from 52w high $222.53 — deep value setup.

balance_sheet.detail: Net cash $1.152B (cash $1.296B - debt $144M), D/E 3.91, current_ratio 2.59 — fortress balance sheet.

dividend.detail: No dividend (payout 0%) — counted as neutral/borderline per framework, not fail.

institutional.detail: Institutional ownership 113.85% (yfinance quirk, effectively >75%), rec_mean 1.79, 13/19 buy or strong_buy.

ai_exposure.detail: EPAM's core business is custom software engineering — exactly the work most exposed to LLM code generation tools. However, the 49.5% drawdown and 8.07x forward PE arguably already discount this risk substantially. Short interest 18.35% confirms bearish AI thesis is consensus.

deal_radar.assessment: 18.35% short interest with 5.48-day cover ratio is the highest in the cohort — heavily contrarian setup with strong squeeze potential if Q1 results show stable bookings.

catalysts_general: Trading $2.63 above 52w low — extreme oversold
12.24% FCF yield is highest in the IT services peer set
PEG 0.67 with 12.8% revenue growth — growth being valued at a discount
$1.15B net cash = 19.4% of market cap acts as embedded floor
Median analyst target $193 = 71.8% upside

downside_scenarios.scenario: Geopolitical re-escalation impacts EPAM's Eastern European delivery centers
AI code generation begins compressing T&M billing rates 10-15%, hits utilization
Severe recession + structural AI disruption — would put PE at trough cyclical levels

ideal_entry.reasoning: Within $1 of 52w low. Entry at $110 implies 6.6% margin to recent low, FCF yield ~13%, and 8.0x earnings on a business with $1.3B cash and 12% revenue growth. Smaller initial position appropriate given AI overhang and 18% short interest indicating risk.

comparables.note: EPAM trades cheaper despite faster growth — but AI/labor-arbitrage risk is more concentrated at EPAM.
EPAM at a 33% PE discount to ACN, justified partly by higher Eastern European exposure but excessive given 12.8% growth rate.
Globant — direct LATAM peer trades at significant premium to EPAM.

value_picks.analyst_take: 5.5/6 framework score with the only blemish being absence of dividend. The contrarian setup is severe: 18.4% short interest, 49.5% drawdown, 8x forward PE, 13% FCF yield, $1.15B net cash, and 12.8% revenue growth that nobody believes. Position sizing should reflect AI labor-arbitrage tail risk — but the price already prices in much of that thesis.

value_picks.name: Accenture plc

fcf_yield.detail: FCF $12.16B / mcap $110.7B = 10.99% — well above 7% threshold for a mega-cap.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 12.06 vs implied PE 16.67 (target_median 248.5 / fwd_eps 14.91) = 27.7% discount — falls in 15-30% borderline band.

drawdown.detail: Down 44.79% from 52w high $325.71 — extreme drawdown for a tier-one mega-cap.

balance_sheet.detail: Net cash $1.06B (cash $9.4B - debt $8.35B), D/E 25.5, current_ratio 1.34, ROE 24.8%.

dividend.detail: Dividend yield 3.63% with payout ratio 51.0% — sustainable and growing.

institutional.detail: Institutional ownership 83.2%, rec_mean 1.86, 18/28 buy or strong_buy.

ai_exposure.detail: Accenture is the world's largest enterprise AI implementation partner — already booked $3B+ in GenAI bookings as of last reported figures and is the bridge between hyperscalers and Fortune 500 deployments. AI is more revenue tailwind than disruption threat at the consulting tier.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest only 3.27% but with +22.5% MoM build — momentum traders piling on the downtrend. Still very low absolute short level signals limited squeeze fuel; thesis here is value/quality, not contrarian flow.

catalysts_general: Trading within $6 of 52w low — rare for a $110B mega-cap to be at the bottom of its 52w range
FCF yield ~11% on a stable consulting franchise with 24.8% ROE
Median target $248.5 = 38.2% upside; high target $320 = 78% upside
Sustainable 3.63% yield while waiting for re-rating
PEG 1.26 reasonable for the highest-quality name in the IT services cohort

downside_scenarios.scenario: Consulting bookings continue to decelerate; lower-end of recent guide
Macro recession trims discretionary consulting spend 10%; AI bookings still growing but base business shrinks
Structural AI disruption thesis — large consultancies lose pricing power as agentic AI handles implementations

ideal_entry.reasoning: Just above 52w low. Entry near $178 captures 3.7% dividend yield, ~11% FCF yield, and 12x forward PE on the highest-ROE name in the cohort. Add aggressively if shares retest $173 (52w low) or break to $165 area.

comparables.note: CTSH cheaper but lower-quality client mix — ACN PE premium is justified by consulting tier and ROE.
IBM trades at premium to ACN despite slower growth — anomalous and supports ACN as relative-value pick.
Indian IT trading at premium to Western consultants is unusual and bullish for ACN re-rating.

value_picks.analyst_take: 5.5/6 score with the only borderline on PE discount (still 27.7%). ACN is the highest-quality name in the cohort by ROE (24.8%) and FCF generation, trading at the absolute 52w low. Tier-one mega-cap consulting franchises rarely sit at 12x forward earnings with an 11% FCF yield — this is the kind of dislocation that usually closes within 6-12 months absent a recession.

value_picks.name: ServiceNow, Inc.

fcf_yield.detail: FCF $5.11B / mcap $94.0B = 5.43% — falls in 4-7% borderline band.

pe_discount.detail: Forward PE 18.22 vs implied PE 27.99 (target_median 140 / fwd_eps 5.00) = 34.9% discount.

drawdown.detail: Down 56.89% from 52w high $211.48 — most severe drawdown in cohort.

dividend.detail: No dividend (payout 0%) — counted as neutral/borderline per framework.

institutional.detail: Institutional ownership 90.5%, rec_mean 1.44 (best in cohort), 43/48 buy or strong_buy.

ai_exposure.detail: ServiceNow's Now Assist GenAI add-on commands premium pricing on top of existing seats — pure expansion vector. Workflow automation is among the highest-leverage enterprise AI use cases. AI is unambiguously a revenue tailwind.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest 3.78% with +39.6% MoM increase — sharpest short build in cohort, but absolute level remains low. Current ratio 0.845 deserves attention — large deferred revenue balance is the explanation for SaaS, not a true liquidity issue.

catalysts_general: Down 56.9% from 52w high — by far the steepest drawdown in cohort
22.1% revenue growth at a 18.2x forward PE = PEG 0.88, exceptional for a 76.5% gross margin software franchise
43 of 48 analysts at buy or strong_buy — highest conviction in cohort (rec_mean 1.44)

downside_scenarios.scenario: Continued SaaS multiple compression; 4-7% slip from current to retest 52w low
Enterprise IT budget freeze + Now Assist attach rates disappoint
GenAI commoditizes workflow software; multiple compresses to legacy enterprise software levels

ideal_entry.reasoning: Watch for retest of 52w low at $81. Entry around $85 gives ~17x forward PE on a 22% growth SaaS franchise with 76% gross margins — historically very rare. Avoid chasing in the $90s; the price action doesn't yet show stabilization.

comparables.note: Salesforce trades at premium with slower growth — NOW dislocation looks anomalous.
INTU is cheaper on PE with comparable growth and dividend — direct relative value alternative.
ADBE has been derated harder — NOW could follow the same path before bottoming.

value_picks.analyst_take: Framework scores 5.0/6 (two borderlines: FCF yield and no dividend) with the highest analyst conviction in the cohort. WATCHLIST rather than BUY because the price action is still in active downtrend (price below SMA20 and SMA50, momentum negative across all timeframes) and the FCF yield doesn't yet cross the 7% pass line. The AI tailwind narrative is real, but better entry likely awaits — wait for stabilization or retest of the $81-85 zone.

deal_radar.assessment: Short interest 3.9% with +30% MoM increase — meaningful short build for a name that historically attracts very little short interest. Sets up well if tax season delivers.

catalysts_general: Down 50.96% from 52w high — extreme for a 23.5% ROE software franchise

ideal_entry.reasoning: Current price $399 is acceptable; ideal staged entry at $380 captures additional ~5% margin. 48.5% earnings growth at 15x forward PE on the leading vertical SaaS franchise is a rare setup. Add on retest of $345-355 zone if available.

comparables.note: NOW grows faster but INTU has higher net margins (21.6% vs 12.6%) and pays a dividend — INTU offers better risk-adjusted exposure.

value_picks.analyst_take: 5.0/6 framework score with both borderlines (FCF yield 4.77% and dividend 1.16%) being soft misses, not hard fails. INTU is the highest-quality compounder in the cohort by margin profile (80.9% gross, 21.6% net) and just-printed 48.5% earnings growth — at 15x forward PE that's the kind of multiple typically seen at cyclical lows for the franchise. The AI thesis here is offensive (more product) rather than defensive (cost out), differentiating it from IT services peers.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Within $3 of 52w low. Entry at $152 captures 2.6% dividend, 6.7% FCF yield, and 14.7x forward PE on the highest-ROE name in the cohort. Regulatory-moat business with sticky recurring revenue from broker-dealer proxy distribution — quality at sale price.

growth_picks.name: Newmont Corporation

capital_efficiency.detail: ROE 25.8% above 20% threshold; capital-intensive miner producing premium returns.

short_dynamics.detail: Short interest 2.03% (<5%) and declining 10.55% MoM — no bear pressure.

ai_exposure.detail: Gold producer with no direct AI exposure; data-center power demand is an indirect macro theme but not a needle-mover for NEM's revenue base.

Mean analyst target $144.01 implies 32.6% upside; 21 analysts, 17 buy/strong-buy

comparables.note: Agnico Eagle is the premium senior peer — typically trades at higher multiple than NEM.
Barrick Gold is the closest scale comparable; NEM's forward P/E 9.6 is in line with sector range.

growth_picks.analyst_take: Newmont is a textbook framework BUY — all six criteria pass with revenue +45.8%, EPS +78.6%, ROE 25.8%, and short interest declining. Forward P/E of 9.58 against $9.8B in FCF suggests the gold-cycle skepticism creates an asymmetric setup for patient holders.

downside_scenarios.scenario: Profit-taking / mean reversion to SMA-50 ($187).
AI-capex digestion concerns or hyperscaler order pace softening.
Hard landing in AI buildout cycle, China export restrictions tighten further, or competitive ASIC erosion.

ideal_entry.reasoning: Around SMA-50 ($187.15), allowing for ~6% pullback to align with prior support; PEG ~0.59 entry zone.

comparables.note: AMD — closest accelerator competitor but materially slower growth and lower margins; NVDA's PEG advantage is substantial.
Broadcom — AI ASIC peer with custom silicon (Google TPU); slower but profitable; trades at similar multiple.

growth_picks.analyst_take: NVIDIA passes 5 of 6 framework criteria cleanly; only short interest (rising from 1.22%) is borderline. With 73% revenue growth, 71% gross margins, and forward P/E 17.66, PEG of 0.63 is anomalous for the AI-infrastructure leader — this is the framework's clearest BUY despite proximity to highs.
